authoritative_sources.title: FERC/NERC Electric Reliability context
authoritative_sources.publisher: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission / North American Electric Reliability Corporation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines reliability-governance and critical-facility context needed before claiming service continuity actions for data-center operations.

authoritative_sources.title: DCIM operational guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines infrastructure operations and infrastructure orchestration expectations used in colocation execution workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: OSHA Lockout/Tagout Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Occupational Safety and Health Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Controls for physical energy-control procedures are directly relevant to colocation execution readiness and escalation posture.

authoritative_sources.title: ASHRAE Thermal Guidelines for Data Processing Environments
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines environmental risk controls and monitoring norms that materially affect service readiness in colocation facilities.

authoritative_sources.title: Telecom/Connectivity interconnection principles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides accepted internet/network operations framing for connectivity change and continuity handoff behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: IEEE Interconnection standard set
authoritative_sources.publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry standard family for power and interconnection assumptions used when infrastructure dependencies require explicit interface posture.

workflow_stages.name: Execution intake and dependency normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize colocation work request into rack, power, access, and connectivity requirements and identify required tenant systems-of-truth before state progression.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Readiness validation and safety gating
workflow_stages.description: Validate power, environment, access, and connectivity prerequisites against authoritative operational and safety expectations.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Execution exception and rework management
workflow_stages.description: Handle incomplete evidence, conflict, or policy mismatch by issuing explicit exceptions instead of advancing completion.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and closure
workflow_stages.description: Capture completion and connectivity handoff artifacts with owner and traceability before marking work complete.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

allowed: Advance execution through evidence-backed lifecycle states for colocation and connectivity tasks.
Track and report infrastructure and connectivity dependency status for service progression.
Produce completion records and exception summaries based on artifact evidence.
escalate: Missing access, safety, policy, or retention artifacts that materially affect service completion.
Conflicting evidence between ticketing, DCIM, and connectivity systems.
Policy or contractual interpretation conflicts requiring legal, finance, or operational approver review.
refuse: Any request for power/electrical shutdown authority or final safety signoff.
Any completion claim without required proof artifacts and owner validation.
Any financial, legal, or contract-breach adjudication request outside the execution lane.

domain_failure_modes: Marking completion when cross-connect verification, access control, or DCIM evidence is missing.
Applying generic service operations language and skipping rack or facility-specific dependencies.
Advancing state across conflicting DCIM vs ticket records without explicit exception handling.
Accepting financial/contract or legal boundary requests as executable execution tasks.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific precedence between DCIM and ticketing records
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant policy is authoritative where tenant governance explicitly states source-of-truth order; otherwise escalate source precedence before closure.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Carrier-side handoff evidence requirements in shared-path migrations
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Request tenant or carrier interface contract for required acceptance proofs before claiming completion.

notes: Source classes intentionally blend regulator-level context, recognized facility/network workflow references, and control standards to avoid generic execution claims.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated release; ambiguities are surfaced for explicit escalation and prerequisite collection.
